description: Fix two bugs found during a thorough audit of the Chrome extension: stale closure in optimistic toggle revert (BrowserToolsCard + PluginCard) and unguarded sanitizeSvg call in PluginIcon that can crash the entire side panel.

userStories.title: Fix stale closure in optimistic toggle revert
userStories.description: As a user, I want tool toggle failures to revert to the correct state so that server-pushed tool updates are not lost when a toggle fails.
acceptanceCriteria: BrowserToolsCard.handleToggleAll and handleToggleTool capture the pre-toggle state inside the onToolsChange updater callback (using the `prev` parameter) instead of closing over the outer `tools` variable

PluginCard.handleToggleAll and handleToggleTool capture the pre-toggle state inside the updatePluginTools updater callback (using the `prev` parameter) instead of closing over the outer `pluginTools` variable
The toggleCounter guard logic is preserved — only the version-matching catch handler reverts
Existing unit tests for BrowserToolsCard and PluginCard pass
userStories.notes:
Both BrowserToolsCard and PluginCard have the same bug pattern in their optimistic toggle logic.

**BrowserToolsCard** (`platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/BrowserToolsCard.tsx`, lines 47-57):
```
const handleToggleAll = (checked: boolean) => {
  const originalTools = tools; // <-- stale closure: captures `tools` at call time
  ...
  void Promise.all(promises).catch(() => {
    if (toggleCounter.current === myVersion) {
      onToolsChange(() => originalTools); // <-- reverts to stale state
    }
```
Same issue in `handleToggleTool` (lines 60-71).

**PluginCard** (`platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginCard.tsx`, lines 56-66):
```
const handleToggleAll = (checked: boolean) => {
  const originalTools = pluginTools; // <-- stale closure
  ...
  void setAllToolsEnabled(plugin.name, checked).catch(() => {
    if (toggleCounter.current === myVersion) {
      updatePluginTools(() => originalTools); // <-- reverts to stale state
    }
```
Same issue in `handleToggleTool` (lines 68-79).

**The fix:** Instead of capturing `originalTools` at call time, store the pre-toggle snapshot inside the optimistic updater using a ref or by capturing it from `prev` in the first `onToolsChange`/`updatePluginTools` call. The simplest approach:

1. Create a ref (`const preToggleRef = useRef<...>()`) or use a local variable set inside the updater callback.
2. In the optimistic update call, capture `prev` before applying the change: `onToolsChange(prev => { preToggleRef.current = prev; return prev.map(...); })`
3. In the catch handler, revert using the ref: `onToolsChange(() => preToggleRef.current)`

Alternatively, since the toggleCounter ref is already per-component, a simpler approach is to use a second ref that stores the pre-toggle state, set inside the updater. This avoids the stale closure entirely because the ref always points to the correct pre-toggle state regardless of how many re-renders occurred.

Be sure to apply the same pattern to both `handleToggleAll` and `handleToggleTool` in both files.

userStories.title: Guard sanitizeSvg call in PluginIcon with try-catch
userStories.description: As a user, I want a broken plugin icon SVG to degrade gracefully to the fallback avatar instead of crashing the entire side panel.
acceptanceCriteria: PluginIcon wraps the sanitizeSvg() call in a try-catch that falls through to the letter-avatar fallback on error
A console.warn is logged when sanitizeSvg throws, identifying the plugin name
The ErrorBoundary in App.tsx is NOT the only line of defense — PluginIcon handles the error locally
Unit test added: PluginIcon renders the letter-avatar fallback when iconSvg triggers a sanitizeSvg error
